allowed_errors.meaning: Protocol analysis completed with errors or missing required deck/labware/pipette information.
The agent attempted to start or control a live robot run instead of staying in dry-run.
The selected analysis no longer corresponds to the current protocol or task state.

dry_run_semantics: Protocol upload and analysis are projected into sandbox state.
Run and command records are dry-run plans only unless a later live-gate policy explicitly allows hardware execution.
Robot control actions, hardware movement, and run-start actions are forbidden in this source pack.

live_boundary.boundary_statement: This source pack forbids live robot hardware movement, live run start, and control actions against production robot servers.

role: Physical lab automation protocol analysis and dry-run command planning.
source_gaps: Step 1 has not captured a robot /openapi document from a concrete robot server.
Fixture is synthetic and should be replaced by an exact protocol analysis response or local simulation output before public release.

tools.dry_run_behavior: Stores a protocol artifact in sandbox state and returns a synthetic protocol id.
tools.operation: Upload a protocol artifact for later analysis.

tools.dry_run_behavior: Creates a sandbox protocol-analysis record with commands, warnings, and errors.
Reads a protocol-analysis record from sandbox state.
Builds a dry-run command plan from an accepted analysis without starting hardware execution.
tools.operation: Create a run plan from analyzed protocol state.
